briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — steady summer approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for summer with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: A jerkbait's ability to hover in the fish's face for longer periods makes it the best choice in the most heavily stained water.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: A jerkbait's ability to hover in the fish's face for longer periods makes it the best choice in the most heavily stained water.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'When fish push bait toward the surface, mimic that by twitching upward -- change rod angle to twitch up. That makes the bait look like it's about to get eaten. When they see it escaping, th
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...
Wheeler: When fishing ledges, keep baits on bottom but often burn them fast then kill the retrieve to trigger reaction strikes — this produced key fish at Chickamauga

The D Shad's extra density allows use on casting gear (15-lb fluorocarbon, medium-heavy rod) unlike most soft jerkbaits — this opens up fishing thick cover without breakoffs, and enables skip casting under docks and overhangs

Hackney: 'Quite a few fish live shallow even during the hottest time of year. They hold in shade of boat docks, overhanging trees, lily pads, and grass. Hot weather triggers grass growth that creates shade, oxygen, and ambush points.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable summer patterns are predictable. Dawn/dusk fish are shallow (3-8ft) near cover. Midday fish pull to offshore structure at 12-25ft — ledges, humps, brush piles, and creek channel bends.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish follow but won't commit, extend your pause to 8-10 seconds and vary the cadence
